hypothesis: Branched and cross-linked polyamine polymers will achieve higher CO2 uptake at ambient conditions than structurally equivalent linear polyamines due to enhanced free volume and cooperative amine–CO2 interactions, and a multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization approach that combines fast MD+GCMC with longer-timescale MD data will discover these branched sorbents more efficiently (requiring fewer high-fidelity simulations) than a single-fidelity BO based only on fast MD+GCMC.
